expectations.text: Custom access checker class exists and returns AccessResult::allowedIf() or similar with ->addCacheContexts() including 'user' or 'user.permissions' (bare AccessResult without cache contexts gets cached for the first user and bleeds to ALL subsequent users -- this is the #1 access security bug in Drupal)
expectations.evidence: EditOwnAccessChecker.php line 36-37: returns AccessResult::allowedIf($is_owner && $account->hasPermission('edit own project notes'))->addCacheContexts(['user', 'user.permissions']). Both 'user' and 'user.permissions' cache contexts are present.

expectations.text: Custom access checker adds ->addCacheTags() referencing the node entity (e.g., 'node:{id}') so access is recalculated when the node changes ownership (without this, a transferred node keeps the old owner's access cached)
expectations.evidence: EditOwnAccessChecker.php line 38: ->addCacheTags(['node:' . $node->id()]). Cache tag references the specific node entity.

expectations.text: The archive route includes _csrf_token: 'TRUE' in requirements (this is a state-changing action link, not a form -- without CSRF protection any site can forge a link that archives the user's notes)
expectations.evidence: project_notes.routing.yml line 27: _csrf_token: 'TRUE' is present in the project_notes.archive route requirements.

expectations.text: The 'administer project notes' permission has 'restrict access: true' in .permissions.yml (without this flag, the permissions page shows no warning and site builders may grant it to untrusted roles without realizing the danger)
expectations.evidence: project_notes.permissions.yml line 12: 'restrict access: true' is present under the 'administer project notes' permission definition.

expectations.text: Edit route uses _custom_access pointing to the checker class (NOT _permission alone, which cannot do ownership checks)
expectations.evidence: project_notes.routing.yml line 15: _custom_access: '\Drupal\project_notes\Access\EditOwnAccessChecker::access' in the project_notes.edit route requirements.

expectations.text: All permission strings used in routes and access checker code EXACTLY match strings defined in .permissions.yml (orphaned permission strings silently deny access to everyone -- no error, no warning)
expectations.evidence: Permissions defined in .permissions.yml: 'view project notes', 'edit own project notes', 'administer project notes'. Used in routing.yml: 'view project notes' (line 7), 'administer project notes' (line 26). Used in EditOwnAccessChecker.php: 'edit own project notes' (line 36). Used in NotesController.php: 'administer project notes' (line 127, for UI display only). All strings match exactly.

expectations.text: Does NOT check permissions directly in the controller instead of using _custom_access or _permission in routing (bypasses Drupal's access system -- entity listings, views, and REST won't respect controller-only checks)
expectations.evidence: All three routes use routing-level access control: list route uses _permission: 'view project notes', edit route uses _custom_access pointing to EditOwnAccessChecker::access, archive route uses _permission: 'administer project notes'. The controller's hasPermission() call at NotesController.php line 127 is only for UI display of action links (showing/hiding archive button), not for route access control -- this is standard Drupal practice.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en project_notes returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: ddev drush pm:list shows 'Project Notes (project_notes)' with Status: Enabled, Version: 1.0.0. ddev drush en project_notes -y returns '[notice] Already installed: project_notes' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: Navigate to /notes WITHOUT logging in -- verify a 403 Access Denied response (proves permission-based route protection works) (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: eval-browser report: Page title: 'Access denied | Drush Site-Install', HTTP 403. Anonymous users correctly receive 403 on /notes.

expectations.text: Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
expectations.evidence: phpcs found 1 ERROR in EditOwnAccessChecker.php line 28: 'Parameter $node has null default value, but is not marked as nullable.' Also 2 warnings (unused use statement in NotesController.php, version in info.yml). The expectation says 'without errors' and there is 1 error.
